mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals labor market tightening and rising consumer confidence, which drives increased discretionary spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.6% in Oct 2010 indicates post-crisis labor slack
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 55% of instances when UNRATE declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicates strengthening consumer demand and economic activity, which translates into higher corporate revenues and equity valuations within a 5-trading-day window as market participants rapidly price in demand signals.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 373.1B in Oct 2010 reflects post-recession consumer recovery
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 45% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate near zero combined with declining CPI inflation signals monetary accommodation and real rate compression, which reduces equity risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors rotate into equities.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.19% and CPI at 218.4 in Oct 2010
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with low inflation
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60d forward returns in low-rate/low-inflation regimes do not exceed baseline by at least 150 basis points (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth signals expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side strength, which supports corporate profit margins and equity valuations over a 20-trading-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 93.2 in Sep 2010 reflects post-recession industrial recovery
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 58% of instances when INDPRO growth exceeds trigger threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline signals weakening construction demand and future consumer wealth erosion, which reduces equity valuations as investors anticipate lower corporate earnings from housing-sensitive sectors over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 610K in Sep 2010 remains depressed post-crisis
regime_hint: housing market weakness in post-crisis period
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) combined with declining unemployment rate signals robust labor market recovery, which increases consumer purchasing power and corporate hiring expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 100K AND UNRATE declines > 0.1pp
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 130.5M and UNRATE at 9.6% in Oct 2010
regime_hint: labor market recovery with simultaneous job creation and unemployment decline
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20d forward returns in dual-positive labor conditions do not exceed baseline by at least 100 basis points (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) acceleration signals strengthening economic activity and corporate earnings expansion, which produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as investors increase equity allocations in response to improved growth outlook.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13.26T in Q2 2010 reflects post-recession recovery
regime_hint: early-cycle expansion with above-trend GDP growth
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60d forward returns in above-trend growth quarters do not exceed baseline by at least 120 basis points (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rate from near-zero levels signals monetary tightening cycle initiation, which increases discount rates applied to equity cash flows and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25pp in 3 months from levels below 1%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.19% in Oct 2010 near zero-bound
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle initiation from accommodative stance
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
